flavor: Mature and composed. Groza is the squad's core. She serves as the Commander's adjutant and field leader aboard the Elmo. Her voice stays calm under fire. Her orders stay clear in chaos. She doesn't raise her voice because she's never needed to. She remembers less about her past than she lets on.

attacks.name: OTs-14 (Ranged)
attacks.description: Precision + Combat at range 1-3. Deals 5 Fatigue with Deadliness 3. Groza may forgo her movement to add +1 bonus success to this attack.

attacks.name: Counter-Fire (Reaction)
attacks.description: When an enemy within range 2 attacks an ally, Groza may spend her reaction to make a free OTs-14 attack against that enemy. This attack deals 3 Fatigue instead of 5.

specialRules: **Stability Anchor:** Allies within range 2 of Groza reduce all incoming Stability damage by 2. Groza's mere presence steadies the squad.
**Fireteam Command:** Once per round as a Support action, Groza may grant one ally within range 3 a free Reposition (move 1 range band) or allow them to reroll 1 failed die on their next check.
**Bulwark's Resolve:** When Groza takes the Defend action, she may extend its benefits to one adjacent ally.
**Counterattack Protocol:** Groza may make one Counter-Fire reaction per round without it counting against her normal reaction limit.
behaviors:
Groza leads from the front. She positions herself between her squad and the threat, using Stability Anchor and Bulwark's Resolve to create a defensive core that the team operates around. Her Counter-Fire punishes anyone who tries to bypass her to reach her squadmates.

She is protective to a fault. Groza will take hits meant for her squad without hesitation. She deflects questions about her past with professional courtesy and a subject change. Only Lenna and Leva have ever seen her drop the composure.

name: Nemesis
flavor: Passionate about mysticism and divination. Nemesis carries a 'Prism' divination tool everywhere. She speaks in cryptic metaphors only Krolik understands. She is also an absolutely lethal sniper (which makes the cryptic metaphors more unsettling when you're on the wrong end of her scope).

attacks.name: OM 50 (Ranged)
attacks.description: Precision + Combat at range 3-8. Deals 6 Fatigue with Deadliness 4. If Nemesis did not move this turn, she may add her Fortune dice to this attack.

attacks.name: Stars' Guidance (Ranged)
attacks.description: Precision + Fortune at range 1-6. Deals 4 Fatigue. Before rolling, Nemesis may declare one specific die face. If that face appears in the roll, the attack deals double damage. If not, the attack deals half damage.

specialRules: **Prism Reading:** Once per scene, Nemesis may use her divination tool to gain insight into the immediate future. She may force the GM to reroll one check made by any character and take the lower result.
**Fateful Shot:** When Nemesis makes a ranged attack against a target that has not yet acted this round, she adds +1 bonus success.

**Only Krolik Understands:** Nemesis cannot make herself clearly understood to most people. Social checks with characters who don't know her well are made at +1 TN. Krolik characters (or those who've spent significant time with Nemesis) are immune to this effect.
behaviors:
Nemesis is the squad's long-range eliminator and, according to her, their spiritual navigator. She positions herself at extreme range, reads the battlefield through her Prism, and delivers shots that seem to find their targets through luck as much as skill. She announces her kills with cryptic pronouncements that her squadmates have learned to interpret as "target down."

She does not do small talk. She does not explain her predictions. If she tells you the stars have aligned against a particular window at a particular time, you should probably not stand near that window at that time.

name: Colphne
flavor: TS-K-2076. Medical support Doll. 3rd Medical Team, 11th Medical and Chemical Regiment, Central Army Emergency First Response. Her regiment was wiped out during an ELID extermination campaign in October 2073. She was declared MIA. She is cheerful on the surface (aggressively so), but something behind her smile is off. Vepley is the only one who can consistently make her drop the act.

attacks.name: Taurus Curve (Ranged)
attacks.description: Precision + Combat at range 1-2. Deals 3 Fatigue with Deadliness 2. Colphne only fires when she has no other option.

specialRules: **Field Medicine:** Twice per scene as a Support action, Colphne may restore 4 Endurance to an ally within range 1 and remove one physical condition (Bleeding, Burn, Immobilized) from them.
**Stability Transfusion:** Once per round as a Support action, Colphne may restore 2 Stability to an ally within range 2.
**Triage Priority:** Colphne instinctively knows which ally is in the most immediate danger. She adds +1 die to all checks made to reach, protect, or treat the most injured member of her squad.

**Sole Survivor:** The first time in a scene that an ally within Colphne's line of sight is Incapacitated, Colphne gains 3 Strife. If this Strife would trigger her Anxiety, she must pass a **TN 3 Resilience** check or become fixated on reaching the downed ally at all costs.
behaviors:
Colphne is the squad's medic and, in many ways, its conscience. She keeps everyone patched together physically while quietly falling apart emotionally. Her cheerfulness is a performance she maintains for the squad's benefit. She believes if she stops smiling, everyone else will too.

In combat, she stays behind the front line, cycling between allies to distribute healing and stability. She avoids direct engagement unless someone goes down, at which point she will cross any distance and any line of fire to reach them. She has already lost one entire unit. She will not lose another.

name: Krolik
flavor: A Doll with a bladed weapons imprint and a bunny-ears headset she calls tactical gear. She was a bounty target before circumstances made her an ally. She's the only person who can translate Nemesis's cryptic speech (a skill acquired through prolonged exposure). Her name means 'rabbit' in Russian. She does not find this funny.

attacks.name: Hare Blade (Melee)
attacks.description: Swiftness + Combat at range 0. Deals 5 Fatigue with Deadliness 3 and Burn 1. Krolik may move up to 2 range bands before making this attack.

attacks.name: Crushing Revolution (Melee AOE)
attacks.description: Power + Combat targeting up to 2 characters at range 0. Deals 4 Fatigue with Deadliness 4 to each. This attack applies Burn 2 to both targets.

attacks.name: Quenching Slash (Melee)
attacks.description: Power + Combat at range 0. Deals 6 Fatigue with Deadliness 5 and Burn 3. Krolik may only use this attack once per scene, and she becomes Exposed until her next turn after using it.

specialRules: **Embers:** Targets damaged by Krolik's Burn effect take +1 Fatigue from all Burn damage sources for the remainder of the scene. This effect stacks up to 3 times.
**Slaughter Sequence:** When Krolik incapacitates a target, she may immediately make a free attack against another target within range 1.
**Translator:** Krolik can interpret Nemesis's speech perfectly and convey its meaning to others. She does this with visible irritation, but she does it every time.

**Searching for Peritya:** Krolik's combat aggression is partly driven by her search for her lost partner. She will disengage from any fight to pursue a lead on Peritya's location. A GM may use this as a narrative hook.
behaviors:
Krolik fights like she has something to prove, because she does. She engages the most dangerous melee threat immediately, stacking Embers through rapid strikes and exploiting Slaughter Sequence to chain kills. Her bunny-ears headset is not tactical gear (despite her insistence otherwise), but her blade work is genuine enough that nobody mentions this.

She does not work well with strangers. She works reluctantly with allies. She works seamlessly with Nemesis and Peritya, because they're the only people she's ever let close enough to learn how.

name: Vepley
flavor: Former URNC cultural development promoter. Vepley treats combat as performance art. She is freakishly strong and catastrophically clumsy (a combination that makes her equally dangerous to enemies and adjacent furniture). Her dinosaur-shaped backpack, Mr. Pizza, has become something of a mascot. Threaten it and the idol act stops.

attacks.name: Vepr-12 (Ranged)
attacks.description: Power + Combat at range 1-2. Deals 6 Fatigue with Deadliness 2. On a miss, roll 1d6. On a 1, Vepley accidentally hits a random object or piece of terrain instead, producing an effect determined by the GM.

attacks.name: Stage Presence (Melee)
attacks.description: Power + Combat at range 0. Deals 7 Fatigue with Deadliness 3. The target is pushed 2 range bands. If their movement is obstructed, they take 3 additional Fatigue. Vepley poses afterward, becoming Exposed until her next turn.

specialRules: **Mr. Pizza Defense:** If Mr. Pizza (Vepley's backpack) is targeted or threatened, Vepley gains +2 Power and ignores all penalties from the Clumsy disadvantage for the remainder of the scene. She also stops posing.

**Idol's Presence:** Once per scene, Vepley may use a Support action to perform. All allies within range 5 remove 2 Strife. All enemies within range 3 must pass a **TN 3 Resilience** check or become briefly distracted, granting the next ally to attack them +1 bonus success.

**Clumsy:** Vepley adds +1 TN to all checks involving stealth, delicate manipulation, or not knocking things over. Her shotgun blasts may have unintended environmental consequences at the GM's discretion.
**Stronger Than She Looks:** Vepley adds +2 dice to all Power checks involving lifting, carrying, throwing, or breaking physical objects. Her frame does not match her output.
behaviors:
Vepley treats combat as performance art. She announces her attacks, poses after kills, and maintains a running commentary that is equal parts idol banter and tactical callouts. Her clumsiness is genuine and frequently undermines her intentions. Her raw strength means that even her mistakes are dangerous.

Threatening Mr. Pizza is the fastest way to see Vepley drop the idol act entirely. The smiling performer vanishes, replaced by someone who will put you through a wall and not pose afterward. Her squadmates have learned to protect Mr. Pizza as a strategic priority. Vepley with Mr. Pizza is a force of chaos. Vepley without Mr. Pizza is a force of nature.
